title: crataegus pedicellata var. albicans
intro: Crataegus pedicellata var. albicans, also known as the White-barked Hawthorn, is a variety of the Crataegus pedicellata plant, which belongs to the Rosaceae family and is commonly found in mountainous regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiotonic effects. The health benefits of White-barked Hawthorn include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. White-barked Hawthorn can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
